seed_problem: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (Frontier-CS problems 0,1; AlphaEvolve).
mutation: composite: keep the max-coverage packing goal but add per-type bounded stock (bounded knapsack) so no single shape can fill the tray, and allow all 8 dihedral orientations; scored against a single-type grid-tiling baseline.
title: Cold-Chain Tray Packing
strategies: single-type grid tiling: pick the one type/orientation with the best bounding-box grid coverage (the baseline B)
area-descending first-fit: scan cells row-major, drop the largest available piece (4 rotations) whose bounding box fits at the anchor
cover-this-cell greedy: for each empty cell try all types and all 8 orientations, shifting a piece so one of its cells lands on the scanned cell, then fill leftover gaps with small pieces
local search / multi-restart over placement order to squeeze coverage toward the tray capacity
